title: Hindola Mahal
tagline: Hindola Mahal — heritage city of Madhya Pradesh

overview.short: The Hindola Mahal (English translation: “Swinging Palace”), is a large meeting hall, or durbar, in the ancient Indian city of Mandu, Madhya Pradesh.
overview.description: The Hindola Mahal (English translation: “Swinging Palace”), is a large meeting hall, or durbar, in the ancient Indian city of Mandu, Madhya Pradesh. Today the Hindola Mahal is a tourist destination in the ruined city.

nearestAirport.name: Devi Ahilya Bai Holkar Airport, Indore

howToReach.roadNote: Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

topPlaces.name: Lohani Caves
topPlaces.description: Lohani Caves are rock-cut caves and temples found near the abandoned Mandu site in southwest Madhya Pradesh, India. These excavations are dated to about the 11th and 12th century CE. The archaeological studies have yielded Hindu statues and carvings such as those of Shiva, Parvati, Vishnu and Lakshmi.

topPlaces.name: Jama Masjid, Mandu
topPlaces.description: The Jama Masjid, also called Jami Masjid, is an historic Friday mosque in Mandu in the Dhar district of the state of Madhya Pradesh, India. Built in Mughal style, the mosque has been believed to have been built during the reign of Hoshang Shah and completed during the reign of Mahmud Khilji in 1454 CE. The Masjid has three large domes, a courtyard, 54 smaller domes and colonnade of pillared halls.

topPlaces.name: Mandav
topPlaces.description: Mandav is a town and a nagar panchayat, near city of Dhar in Dhar district in the Indian state of Madhya Pradesh.

topPlaces.name: Nilkanth temple (Mandu)
topPlaces.description: Nilkanth Temple or Imarat-i-Dilkhusha (the heart-pleasing abode) is a Mughal architecture temple built on the orders of Mughal Emperor Akbar by the governor of Mandu, Shah Badgah in 1574 AD. Akbar commissioned this temple for his favourite wife, Empress Mariam-uz-Zamani, who was a follower of Hinduism. The temple is dedicated to Lord Shiva and is a popular Hindu pilgrimage site.

topPlaces.name: Mandu, Madhya Pradesh
topPlaces.description: Mandu or Mandavgad is an ancient city in the present-day Mandav area of the Dhar district. It is located in the Malwa and Nimar region of western Madhya Pradesh, India, at 35 km from Dhar city.

topPlaces.name: Nalchha, Dhar
topPlaces.description: Nalchha also spelled as Nalcha is a small town in Dhar district in the state of Madhya Pradesh, India. It is situated 25 km away from Dhar, which is both district & sub-district headquarter of Nalchha village.

topPlaces.name: Diesel Loco Shed, Mhow
topPlaces.description: Diesel Loco Shed, Mhow is a metre-gauge engine shed located in Mhow, in the Indian state of Madhya Pradesh. It is located to south of Mhow railway station, it falls under the Ratlam railway division of Western Railway.

items.note: Lohani Caves are rock-cut caves and temples found near the abandoned Mandu site in southwest Madhya Pradesh, India. These excavations are dated to about the 11th and 12th century CE. The archaeological studies have yielded Hindu statues and carvings such as those of Shiva, Parvati, Vishnu and Lakshmi.

The Jama Masjid, also called Jami Masjid, is an historic Friday mosque in Mandu in the Dhar district of the state of Madhya Pradesh, India. Built in Mughal style, the mosque has been believed to have been built during the reign of Hoshang Shah and completed during the reign of Mahmud Khilji in 1454 CE. The Masjid has three large domes, a courtyard, 54 smaller domes and colonnade of pillared halls.
Mandav is a town and a nagar panchayat, near city of Dhar in Dhar district in the Indian state of Madhya Pradesh.

Nilkanth Temple or Imarat-i-Dilkhusha (the heart-pleasing abode) is a Mughal architecture temple built on the orders of Mughal Emperor Akbar by the governor of Mandu, Shah Badgah in 1574 AD. Akbar commissioned this temple for his favourite wife, Empress Mariam-uz-Zamani, who was a follower of Hinduism. The temple is dedicated to Lord Shiva and is a popular Hindu pilgrimage site.
Mandu or Mandavgad is an ancient city in the present-day Mandav area of the Dhar district. It is located in the Malwa and Nimar region of western Madhya Pradesh, India, at 35 km from Dhar city.

Nalchha also spelled as Nalcha is a small town in Dhar district in the state of Madhya Pradesh, India. It is situated 25 km away from Dhar, which is both district & sub-district headquarter of Nalchha village.

Diesel Loco Shed, Mhow is a metre-gauge engine shed located in Mhow, in the Indian state of Madhya Pradesh. It is located to south of Mhow railway station, it falls under the Ratlam railway division of Western Railway.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Hindola Mahal?
faq.a: The best time to visit Hindola Mahal is Oct – Mar. Summers are 26–40°C and winters are 12–28°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Hindola Mahal?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Devi Ahilya Bai Holkar Airport, Indore (58 km) and the nearest railway station is Vadodara Junction (228 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Hindola Mahal?
faq.a: 3 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Hindola Mahal at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Hindola Mahal?
faq.a: Stays in Hindola Mahal start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Hindola Mahal?
faq.a: The top attractions are Lohani Caves, Jama Masjid, Mandu, Mandav, Nilkanth temple (Mandu), Mandu, Madhya Pradesh.
